title: chionochloa rubra
intro: Chionochloa rubra, also known as red tussock grass, is a species of grass native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues and reducing fever. Chionochloa rubra can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions and poultices, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
